name: Old Fashioned Tex-Mex Cheese Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Green Pepper, diced or chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the flour in a pan at about 450°F for 10-15 minutes without burning it.
2. Combine the browned flour with the other dry ingredients and blend with the butter (oil) over low heat until smooth.
3. Slowly add the boiling water, about half a cup at a time, stirring continuously.
4. Add the bouillon cube and stir as you add the remaining water. Simmer the sauce for about one hour, stirring occasionally.
5. Fry the tortillas, one at a time, in hot oil (they should not get crisp, just cook them slightly). Remove from the oil, draining excess oil back into the skillet, and dip them in the hot enchilada sauce.
6. Place each tortilla on a plate, spoon about one tablespoon of onion and green pepper mixture along a line about 1-1/2 inches from the center, place about one tablespoon of cheese along that line, and carefully roll the tortilla around the filling with the seam side down in a rectangular baking dish.
7. Repeat with all the tortillas.
8. Pour any remaining sauce over the enchiladas and spread some onions/peppers on top.
9. Finally, sprinkle generous portions of grated cheese on top. Bake at about 300°F for 15-20 minutes. Do not overcook!
10. Optionally, add spiced cooked ground beef or boneless chicken to the filling, reducing onion/pepper/cheese by half and adding one tablespoon of meat filling.
